title: How Alien: Romulus Box Office Opening Compares to the Entire Alien Franchise
text: Alien: Romulus opened with $42 million domestically, marking the second-strongest debut in franchise history. The film trails only 2012's Prometheus, which earned $51 million during its opening weekend over a decade ago. The article notes that earlier installments like the original Alien and Aliens cannot be fairly compared due to limited theater counts. Among other sequels, only Alien vs. Predator ($38 million in 2004) and Alien: Covenant ($36 million in 2017) exceeded $30 million in opening weekend earnings. Director Fede Alvarez discussed potential sequel prospects with The Hollywood Reporter. He explained that "for me, it's always been about story," and that the filmmaking team naturally began contemplating narrative extensions after completing production. Alvarez suggested they pondered what awaited characters upon reaching their destination, acknowledging such thoughts "sounds like a sequel." The strong opening demonstrates confidence in the franchise's future and the filmmaking approach taken by Alvarez.
